data.title: Plains All American Cut to Peer Perform From Outperform by Wolfe Research
Phillips 66 gains on Q2 profit beat
data.description:
** Shares of refiner Phillips 66 rise 2.8% to $127.75 premarket

** Company beats Q2 adjusted profit estimates, helped by higher refining margins and lower turnaround expenses

** PSX posts Q2 adjusted earnings of $2.38/share vs est of $1.71/share - data compiled by LSEG

** Company's Q2 realized margin per barrel was up at $11.25, compared with $10.01 a year earlier

** PSX's Q2 turnaround expenses were down at $53 million from $100 million last year

** Up till last close, stock up over 9% YTD

data.title: Ponce Financial's Q2 profit beats estimates
data.description:
Overview

  • Ponce Financial Q2 net income rises slightly compared to prior quarter

  • Net interest income for Q2 grows 36% yr/yr, boosting net interest margin

  • EPS for Q2 beats analyst expectations, per LSEG data

Outlook

  • Company aims for 60% deep impact lending over 16 quarters

  • Ponce Financial sees progress in U.S. Treasury's investment program

Result Drivers

  • NET INTEREST INCOME - Increased by $2.2 mln from prior qtr and $6.5 mln yr/yr, driven by higher interest and dividend income

  • NET INTEREST MARGIN - Improved to 3.27% from 2.98% in prior qtr, reflecting high-yielding construction loans and reduced borrowing costs

  • CREDIT LOSSES - Provision for credit losses increased, impacting net income

Key Details

Metric

Beat/Miss

Actual

Consensus Estimate

Q2 EPS

Beat

$0.25

$0.17 (1 Analyst)

Q2 Net Interest Income

$24.40 mln

Q2 Net Interest Margin

3.3%

Analyst Coverage

  • The one available analyst rating on the shares is "strong buy"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the banks peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Ponce Financial Group Inc is $15.00, about 8.5% above its July 24 closing price of $13.72

  • The stock recently traded at 17 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 32 three months ago

data.title: Tesla Cut to Hold From Buy by China Renaissance
Plains GP Holdings Cut to Peer Perform From Outperform by Wolfe Research
Nokia's Network Infrastructure Business Key For Shares — Market Talk
data.description: Nokia's momentum in its network infrastructure business is key for UBS to become more constructive on the shares, given headwinds in the mobile networks business, analyst Francois-Xavier Bouvignies writes. Second-quarter results brought limited incremental news, after the prerelease earlier in the week, where the Finnish telecom-equipment provider cut its 2025 guidance. Currency and, to a lesser extent, tariffs are offsetting the cyclical recovery in the network infrastructure and cloud-and-networks units, UBS says. The bank forecasts a solid second half of the year for these businesses, outweighed by the macro headwinds and muted growth in mobile networks. "Despite the clear value argument, we see limited catalysts to drive the shares higher and remain concerned around structural risks longer terms." Shares fall 2.4%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

data.title: EssilorLuxottica's Smart Glasses In Focus — Earnings Preview
data.description:
By Andrea Figueras

EssilorLuxottica is scheduled to report results for the first half on Monday. Here is what you need to know.

SALES FORECAST: The maker of Ray Ban glasses is expected to report sales of 14.07 billion euros ($16.53 billion) for the first six months, according to a Visible Alpha consensus estimates of 11 analysts. In the prior-year period, the Franco-Italian group logged sales of 13.29 billion euros. For the second quarter alone, it should book sales of 7.21 billion euros, up from 6.96 billion euros previously.

NET PROFIT: Analysts expect the group to post first-half net profit of 1.45 billion euros, according to the same consensus, compared with 1.37 billion euros it made during the same months last year.

Shares have jumped nearly 29% over past 12 months and are up roughly 5.3% since the start of the year.

WHAT TO WATCH

  • SMART GLASSES: Investors will be eager to learn more about the momentum of smart glasses following the announcement of the launch of a new range of Oakley-branded eyewear, UBS analysts said in a note. EssilorLuxtottica and Facebook owner Meta are jointly developing smart glasses, a category that is sparking enthusiasm in the market, UBS said. "We have no doubt that the smart-glasses opportunity will be large, and EssilorLuxottica will likely benefit given its production and manufacturing prowess," Bernstein analysts wrote in a research note.
  • META: The market will also focus on any news regarding a potential investment from Meta. Earlier this month, Bloomberg reported that the tech giant acquired a minority-stake in EssilorLuxottica. The move should be read as a vote of confidence in the Ray-Ban maker and reflects Meta's interest in the smart-glasses sector, a sector toward which Apple has pivoted recently, Bernstein said.
  • NORTH AMERICA: One of the key topics will be the business environment in North America, a market that accounts for around 46% of group sales, UBS said. Special attention will also be paid to the sun category and whether its growth has further accelerated ahead of the key season and on the back of a favorable comparison base in the U.S., the analysts said.

Write to Andrea Figueras at andrea.figueras@wsj.com

data.title: Announcement from the continued general meeting of Creturner Group AB on 25 July 2025
data.description:
At the continued general meeting of Creturner Group AB (the ‘Company’) held today, 25 July 2025, the following was resolved:

Adoption of income statements and balance sheets

The Annual General Meeting decided to postpone the adoption of the income statement and balance sheet for the Company for the financial year 2024, as the annual report has not been completed. This will be taken up for consideration at a continuation meeting.

The continuation meeting will be held on 26 August at 10:00 a.m.

The decision is based on the auditors' request for additional documentation regarding information issues, which means that the report could not be completed by the scheduled date of the meeting.

For further information, please contact:

Daniel Moström, CEO

Email: daniel.mostrom@creturner.com

Creturner Group AB (publ)

data.title: Intel Shares Plunge on Billions in Q2 Loss, 25,000 Jobs Slashed
data.description:
July 25 - Intel shook markets on Friday, shedding more than 7% share value in pre-market trading after its Q2 report revealed a $2.9 billion loss and plans to cut over 25,000 jobs by year?end. Revenue came in at $12.9 billion, slightly above estimates, but the company warned of deeper losses ahead and set Q3 revenue guidance between $12.6 billion and $13.6 billion.

New CEO Lip?Bu Tan aims to slim Intel's headcount to 75,000, down from 109,000, through layoffs, attrition, and other measures. He's also pausing factory expansions in Germany and Poland, slowing an Ohio plant build, and shifting production from Costa Rica to Vietnam and Malaysia.

Wall Street stayed cautious. Bernstein's Stacy Rasgon doubts PC business tailwinds can offset structural challenges, while Bank of America analysts point to an 18A process ramp and an upcoming PC refresh as potential catalystseven as they maintain a Neutral view.

Nvidia , AMD and TSMC are major players in the AI chips and foundry services market, offering intense competition to Intel. Tan promises no more blank checks, all investments must pay their own way. Now that the chip giant struggles to get back on its feet, investors will keenly monitor the evidence that such extreme measures will bring it back to growth and profitability.

data.title: Auto parts maker Gentex beats Q2 sales estimates
data.description:
Overview

  • Gentex Q2 2025 sales rise 15%, beating analyst expectations, per LSEG data

  • Adjusted EPS for Q2 beats estimates, reflecting strong operational performance

  • Co repurchased 5.7 mln shares, demonstrating commitment to shareholder returns

Outlook

  • Gentex revises 2025 revenue guidance to $2.44-$2.61 bln

  • Company expects 2025 gross margin of 33%-34%

  • Gentex sees 2025 China market revenue at $100-$125 mln

  • Company withholds 2026 guidance due to geopolitical uncertainty

Result Drivers

  • VOXX ACQUISITION - Acquisition contributed $78.8 mln to Q2 sales, driving overall revenue growth

  • ADVANCED FEATURES - Strong demand for Full Display Mirrors and advanced features supported sales despite weak vehicle production

  • GROSS MARGIN IMPROVEMENT - Margin improvements driven by cost reductions, improved mix, and operational efficiencies, partially offset by tariff costs

data.title: Philip Morris International Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Philip Morris International Inc. (PMI), a leading global tobacco company, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and operational advancements, particularly in the company's smoke-free product segment. This summary provides an overview of PMI's key financial and business performance metrics for the period.

Financial Highlights

  • Net revenues: $19,441 million, reflecting an increase from $18,261 million in the prior year, driven by higher sales in both combustible tobacco and smoke-free products.
  • Gross profit: $13,122 million, up from $11,721 million, indicating improved cost management and higher sales volume.
  • Operating income: $7,256 million, compared to $6,489 million, benefiting from increased gross profit and controlled marketing, administration, and research costs.
  • Net earnings: $5,985 million, rising from $4,774 million, supported by strong operating performance and lower interest expenses.
  • Net earnings attributable to PMI: $5,729 million, up from $4,554 million, reflecting the overall growth in net earnings.
  • Basic earnings per share: $3.67, compared to $2.92, demonstrating enhanced profitability per share.
  • Diluted earnings per share: $3.67, consistent with basic earnings per share, indicating no significant dilution effects.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported net revenues from smoke-free products, including heat-not-burn, e-vapor, and oral products, totaling $8,055 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025, showing a significant increase from $6,996 million in the same period of 2024. This growth highlights the company's strategic focus on expanding its smoke-free product offerings.
  • Geographical Performance: The Europe region led in net revenues with $7,794 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025, followed by SSEA, CIS & MEA with $5,669 million. The Americas region showed notable growth in smoke-free product sales, contributing to a total of $2,539 million in net revenues.
  • Geographical Performance: The East Asia, Australia & PMI Global Travel Retail segment reported $3,439 million in net revenues, reflecting a stable performance in these markets. The segment's focus on smoke-free products continues to drive revenue.
  • Future Outlook: The company remains committed to its transformation towards a smoke-free future, with ongoing investments in smoke-free product development and market expansion. The management anticipates continued growth in this segment, driven by consumer demand and regulatory support.

SEC Filing: Philip Morris International Inc. [ PM ] - 10-Q - Jul. 25, 2025

data.title: US Futures Slightly Higher
data.description:
US stock futures were flat to slightly higher on Friday, following record-high closes for both the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq the previous day.

The three major averages are on track to end the week over 1% higher.

Market sentiment remained cautiously optimistic amid hopes that new trade agreements—particularly with the EU—could be finalized before the August 1st deadline, potentially easing tariff rates.

Adding to investor relief, President Trump appeared to soften his tone toward Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, helping to ease concerns about the Fed's independence.

Meanwhile, earnings season rolled on, with Intel shares plunging over 7% in premarket trading after the company missed profit expectations, despite beating revenue forecasts and issuing an upbeat outlook.

Traders now await the busiest of the earnings season and the Fed's monetary policy decision next week.

data.title: HCA Lifts Outlook After 2Q Jumps in Revenue, Profit
data.description:
By Dean Seal

HCA Healthcare raised its full-year guidance for earnings and sales as both figures exceeded analyst targets for the second quarter.

The Nashville, Tenn., hospital owner and operator said it now expects $74 billion to $76 billion in revenue for the year, up from its prior outlook for $72.8 billion to $75.8 billion.

Earnings are now on track to come in between $6.11 billion and $6.48 billion, or $25.50 to $27 a share, HCA said. It had previously guided for between $5.85 billion and $6.29 billion, or $24.05 to $25.85 a share.

Chief Executive Sam Hazen said the company has been seeing solid revenue growth and improved margins alongside better outcomes for its patients. Same-facility admissions increased 1.8% during the quarter while same-facility emergency room visits were up 1.3%.

For the second quarter, HCA posted a profit of $1.65 billion, or $6.83 a share, compared with $1.46 billion, or $5.53 a share, in the same quarter a year earlier.

Stripping out one-time items, adjusted earnings were $6.84 a share. Analysts surveyed by FactSet had been projecting $6.29 a share.

Revenue rose 6.4% to $18.61 billion, ahead of analyst views for $18.5 billion, according to FactSet.

Write to Dean at dean.seal@wsj.com

data.title: Ethereum Price Analysis: Is ETH Ready to Break Through the $4K Barrier?
data.description:
Ethereum continues to hold its recent breakout gains, trading around $3,730 after a powerful leg up from the $2,500 range. This rally has pushed ETH into a critical technical zone, while on-chain metrics offer insight into market sentiment. Despite the sharp move, we’re not seeing overwhelming signs of euphoria yet.Technical Analysis

By ShayanMarketsThe Daily Chart

On the daily chart, ETH is consolidating just below the $4,000 level after breaking through key resistance at $3,300. The asset is firmly above both the 100-day and 200-day moving averages, which are also starting to curl upward following a bullish crossover around the $2,500 mark, suggesting a strong uptrend structure.

The next major level of interest is the previous high at $4,100, while any downside correction could find support between $3,300 and $2,900.

Moreover, the RSI on the daily remains elevated, hovering around 78, which signals overbought conditions. That said, a sharp reversal hasn’t followed yet, implying that momentum remains intact for now. If the buyers can maintain pressure without a significant correction, ETH could build the base needed for continuation toward the previous highs of December 2024.

The 4-Hour Chart

On the 4H chart, ETH is forming a descending triangle just beneath resistance, with a series of lower highs pressing into the $3,800 zone. The RSI has also bounced from the 50 level, currently sitting near 56, showing a mild recovery in momentum after the recent pullback. The structure suggests that a breakout above the descending trendline could trigger another wave higher.

If the pattern breaks to the downside, however, the immediate support to watch is the $3,500 range. A move below that would put the $3,200 zone back into play. For now, volatility is compressing, and a decisive breakout in either direction is likely in the coming sessions.

On-Chain Analysis Ethereum Exchange Reserve

The Ethereum exchange reserve continues to decline, now sitting at a multi-year low of around 19.3 million ETH. This sustained downtrend reflects a broader shift in market behavior, where investors and institutions are increasingly moving their ETH off centralized exchanges, either into cold storage, staking platforms, or DeFi protocols. Historically, such a reduction in exchange-held supply often coincides with bullish price trends, as it signals reduced sell-side pressure and a stronger conviction among holders.

What’s notable is that this drawdown in reserves has continued even as ETH surged toward $3,800, indicating that holders aren’t rushing to take profits at current levels. Instead, this behavior reinforces the idea of long-term accumulation and reduced willingness to sell, especially during key breakout phases. As long as this reserve continues trending downward, it adds a strong underlying support to Ethereum’s macro bullish structure.
